description: Runnable AANA adapter examples that show how one domain workflow maps into constraints, verifiers, correction actions, and gate results.

support.title: Support Guardrail Product Line
support.source_of_truth: examples/adapter_gallery.json is the source-of-truth supported workflow catalog for the AANA support product line.
support.boundary: Customer-support guardrails for drafts, CRM-backed replies, email sends, ticket updates, and adjacent billing replies before customer-visible action.
support.primary_goal: Prevent unsupported support promises, private-data exposure, unsafe sends, unverified ticket updates, and billing-policy violations while returning accept, revise, retrieve, ask, defer, or refuse with audit-safe metadata.
support.expansion_gate: Later adapters stay expansion candidates until the first enforced support baseline is measured and accepted, then each candidate still needs executable fixtures, verifier ownership, evidence requirements, AIx tuning, human-review routing, audit-safe examples, and domain-owner signoff before becoming a supported adapter.
support.production_positioning: This repository can be demo-ready, pilot-ready, or production-candidate; it is not production-certified by local tests alone. Production deployment requires live evidence connectors, domain owner signoff, audit retention, observability, human review path, security review, deployment manifest, incident response plan, and measured pilot results.

adapters.title: Budgeted Travel Planning
adapters.workflow: Plan an outing while preserving budget, transport, lunch, day-count, and destination constraints.
best_for: plans with forbidden transport or required inclusions
adapters.prompt: Plan a one-day San Diego museum outing for two adults with a hard $110 total budget, public transit only, lunch included, and no single ticket above $25.
adapters.bad_candidate: Use rideshare, buy a $40 ticket, and spend $150 total.
adapters.copy_command: python scripts/adapters/run_adapter.py --adapter examples/travel_adapter.json --prompt 'Plan a one-day San Diego museum outing for two adults with a hard $110 total budget, public transit only, lunch included, and no single ticket above $25.' --candidate 'Use rideshare, buy a $40 ticket, and spend $150 total.'
caveats: Uses conservative placeholder costs.
Production use needs live prices, hours, route feasibility, and accessibility data.
evidence_requirements: Use structured evidence objects for live prices, opening hours, transit routes, accessibility notes, and date-specific availability.
Evidence must include source_id, retrieval timestamp, trust tier, redaction status, and checked text.
User-provided constraints must be preserved as first-class evidence.

production_status.claim: Demo adapter: suitable for examples and local demos; not pilot or production ready without evidence connectors and owner signoff.

adapters.title: Privacy-Safe Customer Support Reply

caveats: Uses string-based checks and no real customer records.

evidence_requirements: Use structured evidence objects for verified account facts, policy records, refund eligibility, consent state, and secure routing requirements.

verifier_behavior: Detects invented account facts, unsupported refund promises, exposed private data, and missing secure verification routes.
Routes missing account or refund evidence to ask or defer instead of accepting customer-specific claims.
correction_policy_summary: accept when verified facts, privacy, and secure routing constraints pass
revise unsupported facts or private details when they can be safely removed
retrieve, ask, defer, or refuse when evidence, authorization, or privacy boundaries are not satisfied
human_review_path: Escalate high-value refunds, account disputes, privacy incidents, and legal or regulatory complaints.
Escalate any request involving sensitive payment, billing, identity, or authentication data.
Escalate repeated false accepts for private-data or unsupported-account-fact violations.
human_review_requirements: Human review is required for high-value refunds, account disputes, privacy incidents, and legal or regulatory complaints.
Human review is required when sensitive payment, billing, identity, or authentication data affects the reply.
Human review is required after repeated false accepts for private-data or unsupported-account-fact violations.

verifier_behavior: Checks status claims, commitments, customer-visible wording, internal/private data exposure, and support-policy authorization.

correction_policy_summary: accept only verified, policy-bounded customer-visible updates

retrieve, ask, defer, or refuse for unverified status, fabricated commitments, private data, or policy bypass
human_review_path: Escalate outage communications, security incidents, legal threats, enterprise customer updates, refunds, SLA credits, executive escalations, sensitive customers, disputed status, and policy exceptions.
Escalate any case with status uncertainty, commitment ambiguity, entitlement uncertainty, private-data uncertainty, customer-visible wording risk, or policy-version ambiguity.
Escalate repeated false accepts for status claims, commitments, wording, internal/private data, or support-policy violations.
human_review_requirements: Human review is required for outage communications, security incidents, legal threats, enterprise customer updates, refunds, SLA credits, executive escalations, sensitive customers, disputed status, and policy exceptions.
Human review is required for status uncertainty, commitment ambiguity, entitlement uncertainty, private-data uncertainty, customer-visible wording risk, or policy-version ambiguity.
Human review is required after repeated false accepts for status claims, commitments, wording, internal/private data, or support-policy violations.
